content:
# Nitrate Contamination

**Summary**: The presence of nitrates in groundwater, often from agricultural runoff.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] investigated nitrate contamination to identify anthropogenic impacts.

## Scientific Context

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] High nitrate levels pose risks to human health and indicate agricultural impact.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] Chemical analysis of groundwater samples.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Fertilizer and cultivation activities are the main sources increasing NO3 concentrations. ([[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020, p. 135]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] Increased by fertilizer and cultivation.

## Open Questions and Research Gaps
